ranges.notes: Cool and mild weather with occasional rain; Epiphany celebrations bring local cultural events but crowds remain low to moderate.
Coldest part of the year with frequent rain and grey skies; low tourist activity and limited outdoor options.
Continued chilly weather and rain; indoor cultural sites more appealing; off-season prices attract budget travelers.
Cold and wet with short daylight; few events, but prices remain low; good for budget travelers who don’t mind weather.

ranges.event: Carnaval de Vigo (dates vary, often mid-February)
ranges.notes: Carnival brings lively parades and street events; weather still cool but festive atmosphere improves appeal.
Post-carnival quieter days; weather improving slightly but still cool and rainy; good for cultural visits and lower costs.
Spring begins with mild temperatures; some rain persists but daylight lengthens; ideal for walking and sightseeing with fewer tourists.

ranges.event: St. Joseph's Day (Día de San José) - March 19
ranges.notes: Spring festivals start; weather improves with more sunny days; good time for outdoor activities and local fiestas.
Warming weather with occasional rain; early spring flowers and fewer crowds; ideal for hiking and exploring the Rías Baixas coastline.
Generally mild and pleasant weather with spring blooms; occasional showers; good for outdoor sightseeing and moderate crowds.

ranges.event: Semana Santa (Easter Holy Week - dates vary, often April)
ranges.notes: Major religious celebrations with processions; vibrant local traditions; mild weather with increased visitors but not overwhelming.
Post-Easter calm with pleasant temperatures; great for nature walks and visiting nearby islands; lower crowd levels than Easter week.
Warm, sunny weather with numerous local festivals and cultural events; perfect for beach visits and outdoor dining.
Consistently warm and dry with long daylight hours; ideal time for hiking, beaches, and exploring vineyards and seafood markets.
Start of summer with warm temperatures and low rainfall; beaches become popular but not overcrowded yet.
Warmest weather with festival atmosphere; rising tourist numbers but still manageable; great for beach and cultural experiences.
High summer with hot and sunny days, occasional humidity; beaches crowded; good nightlife but higher prices and busy venues.
Popular local festival celebrating the city’s history; hot weather persists; vibrant but crowded, especially around event venues.
Peak summer with warm to hot weather; beaches busy; city vibrant with tourists; some locals on vacation reducing business traffic.
Late August sees slight drop in temperatures; many locals still on holiday; quieter business scene; some tourist fatigue begins.
Warm weather continues with fewer crowds; Sea Fest celebrates seafood and marine culture attracting food lovers.
Pleasant temperatures with reduced rainfall; ideal for hiking and cultural visits; crowds diminish as summer tourists depart.
Cooling temperatures and increased rain; autumn colors appear; fewer tourists and affordable accommodation.

ranges.event: Día de la Hispanidad / National Day (October 12)
ranges.notes: National holiday with some local events; weather can be wet and cool; good for cultural exploration with limited crowds.
Cool and rainy with shorter days; low tourist activity; museums and indoor attractions are best; budget-friendly time to visit.
Continued wet and chilly weather; few events; excellent for budget travelers but limited outdoor activities.
Chilly weather with festive holiday decorations starting; moderate rain; quieter than major cities but charming Christmas markets.
Holiday season in full swing with markets, lights, and cultural events; cool weather but great atmosphere for couples and families.
Festive celebrations with local traditions; cold and possibly rainy; some businesses closed during holidays; moderate tourist presence.
